patient_utterances: [Clinician asked: "Hi there, it's Doctor Smith from Babylon."] Hello. Hi there.

[Clinician asked: "Hi, can you confirm your name, date of birth and your home address, please?"] Yeah, it's Matt Carter, and it's fourteenth of August, nineteen eighty nine, and  what was the last bit?
[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> Your address."] And it's one Babylon Avenue.

[Clinician asked: "And are you in a private place where you can have a consultation today? What can I do for you?"] Yes. Um,  coming, I, I don't really know what's been going on, but it's just aching all over, but,  definitely the last six weeks, it might have been slightly longer than that, but it's just sort of that, feeling really tired, run down.

[Clinician asked: "OK, um, so when do you think you were last well?"] Uh, where are we now, we've just started April. I, definitely Christmas, New Year, I felt relatively well, I think. Um, so, since then. Actually, yeah, I, I was on holiday in, um, in the States. And yeah,  it probably coincided with some time around then. End of January, start of Feb.
[Clinician asked: "So do you think it started when you were on holiday or after you got back?"] Uh, probably a couple of weeks after I got back.
[Clinician asked: "Were you well when you were on holiday?"] Yeah, had a had a great time, very active, uh.
[Clinician asked: "Did you get any infections, chest infections, diarrhoea, vomiting, anything like that?"] Um, no, not that I recall. Um, yeah, not. No, the symptoms sort of all started afterwards.

[Clinician asked: "OK. So, just tell me what symptoms you've been having, in terms of just your general health. Any persistent cold symptoms or earaches, sore throat, chest  cough, shortness of breath, phlegm, anything like that?"] Uh, no.
[Clinician asked: "Any change in your weight at all?"] Uh, I don't think so. Like, I'm probably less hungry, but no, no weight change, just a bit.
[Clinician asked: "OK, and are you eating different amounts?"] Uh, yeah, probably slightly smaller portions, or it, yeah, more of an effort to, to actually eat everything that's in front of me.
[Clinician asked: "Any difficulty swallowing or acid in your throat, or abdominal pain?"] No.
[Clinician asked: "Any change in your bowel habit, diarrhoea, vomiting or blood in the stool?"] Uh, no.
[Clinician asked: "Any difficulty passing urine or blood in the urine?"] No, that's all fine.

[Clinician asked: "OK, and any other general symptoms like rashes? Or."] Um, I don't have any rashes now, but there was, uh, in fact I've got a photo, um, on my phone if if that's helpful. I can try and show you, hang on. Um, because this, yeah, this was weird, this sort of came up, and then, uh.
[Clinician asked: "OK. OK."] Disappeared after a couple of weeks, where's the light,  that, you see that?
[Clinician asked: "OK, right, yeah, whereabouts was it?"] Uh, I think sort of calf, back of my calf, because I didn't notice it, my wife noticed it actually.

[Clinician asked: "When did you first notice it?"] Um, probably, uh, around the end of February, the middle to end of Feb. And then it, like, it didn't actually cause too much itching, or anything, at the time. And, I, it faded over a couple of weeks, I guess.

[Clinician asked: "Did you have any fevers at that time?"] Um, I, to be honest that actually turns out it was a bit of a sore throat I had, um, so possibly a little bit feverish, but I, I sort of put it down to that.

[Clinician asked: "Do you know where you got that, when it started? Had you been walking, did you get any bites that you noticed?"] Uh, I  like,  I do quite a lot of walking, and we did, um, quite a lot while we were out in the, on holiday as well, but definitely, um. Yeah, I mean, I don't, I don't recall any bugs, I don't remember seeing anything bitten but you often, like, yeah, as you're walking through, I was in shorts and, and boots but <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>/
[Clinician asked: "OK. Whereabouts were you in the States?"] Um, just sort of, um, he examined me, so.
[Clinician asked: "OK, and any headaches, blurred vision, tingling, numbness?"] Um, no. But it's the sort of headache maybe in just generally feeling a bit crappy. <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>
[Clinician asked: "And have you been able to go to work as usual?"] Um, I've been going to work. I don't know how effective it's been. But, yeah, been, been.
[Clinician asked: "And what's your job?"] Um, I work for a health technology company actually.

[Clinician asked: "OK, and has your concentration been affected, or your tiredness levels in the daytime at work?"] Yeah, definitely sort of tired and, um, yeah, less energy, sort of probably <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> than I, I would normally be.

[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> Have you been able to exercise OK?"] Um. Not as much. I, I don't know if it's, I haven't been able to or I've just been a bit lazy with it. I  I'm not exercising as I would normally.

[Clinician asked: "OK, fine, and, um, how's your mental health, your stress levels, all that?"] Hey. Pretty good at the moment, actually. Like, it previously that has been an issue, but, um. Other than, sort of, physically feeling a bit. Uh, crappy, I've. Yeah, in a pretty good place.

[Clinician asked: "OK. Apart from that rash, any other rashes on your skin, or swelling of your joints, or pain in your joints?"] Uh, yeah, sort of aching shoulders, back, the hips, knees, but sort of, yeah. Not ,
[Clinician asked: "Any  swelling that you've noticed?"] No, .
[Clinician asked: "Your hands, wrists, ankles, toes."] Uh, no.
[Clinician asked: "OK, um, and any other significant medical problems in the past?"] Uh, I mean. <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>
[Clinician asked: "Pardon?"] Asthma and hay fever if they can help.
[Clinician asked: "OK. Any flare-ups of your asthma recently?"] Uh, no,  pretty well , I think.
[Clinician asked: "Do you take your regular inhalers or other medication?"] Uh, yeah. I've got one of those, um, preventative, I think. Yeah <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>, but I've not used one.
[Clinician asked: "You haven't been using it, you said?"] I, I have it. I've been using the  everyday for the morning and night but not the, uh, .
[Clinician asked: "OK. And any, erm, do you take any other medications?"] Um,  I take, um, one of the antihistamines  it might be  can I get that from, just from the pharmacy.
[Clinician asked: "Yeah, that's fine. Um, and do you smoke?"] uh, .
[Clinician asked: "Drink any alcohol? No."] Sorry, occasionally, that was.
[Clinician asked: "OK, and who do you live with at home?"] Uh, just my wife.

[Clinician asked: "Um. Sorry, um, so with this rash, we would need to investigate whether this has been a contributing factor to your symptoms. And just because of your symptoms, which have been ongoing for six weeks, that's obviously quite a long time to notice a change in your energy levels. Um, I think it's importa"] <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> Uh, yeah, I <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> but, uh, what, what's the infection?

[Clinician asked: "Well, you can get, uh, you can get a bacterial infection caused which is called, um, it's called lyme disease that you can get a rash like that from a tick bite. So that's something we would do a test for, as well. Um, and if that comes back positive, then there are specific antibiotics that we woul"] OK. OK.
[Clinician asked: "So it's definitely worth checking for, but we would also check just for  which can cause reduced energy, tiredness and aching for, persistent over six weeks."] OK.

[Clinician asked: "So if you're happy to do that, if you book that through the support line, and then when you've had the test arrange a follow up about a week after, after you've had the test done."] OK, so, do the test first and then,  and that follow up we can just do over the phone again? Or,  yeah.
[Clinician asked: "Um, yeah, we can do that over the phone to start with."] All right. Excellent.
[Clinician asked: "OK?"] That, that's really helpful, thank you very much.
[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>"] Oops, sorry, you broke up a bit there.
[Clinician asked: "Any other questions?"] Ohh, no, not , no.
[Clinician asked: "OK, that's great. Take care."] OK, take care, thank you, bye.
gold_note:
PC: Tired all the time.

HPC: Tired and feeling run down for last 6 weeks. Last felt well at xmas. Seemed to start after return from holiday in the States. Did lots of walking in shorts in Yosemite, didn't notice bite. Well when on hol. Felt unwell on return. Initially had red circular rash on back of calf mid-end Feb.  Lasted few weeks and faded. Had fever, sore throat at that time. Reduced appetite, no wt loss, 

SE: GI - Dec app, no change in BH, no blood in stool, no dyspepsia.
Neuro: General headache/no visual disturbance/altered sens/weakness.Yosemite Park. 
RS: No cough/sputum/CP/SOB. No coryzal sx/sore throat.
Not exercising due to lack of energy
LS: Aching shoulder/legs/back. No joint swelling.
No other rashes noted.

No sx depression.

PMH: Asthma, hayfever

DH: Has bd inhaler ? ICS, does not take salbutamol regularly. Cetirizine OD
Occ.Lives with wife. Non smoker. Occ alcohol.

Imp: 6/52 hx fatigue and hx circular rash on calf.

Plan: Needs screening bloods for TATT including Lyme serology.

FU 1 week later with GP.
10.  13:30-13:50
		“I have a blinding headache”
Note: PC : Headache

HPC: 3/7 hx headache. Starts on waking up. Over right eye. Seeing lights in front of eyes. Can be worse when stands up not when bends over/cough/sneeze. Vision not disturbed. No nausea/vomiting. No altered taste/smell. Worse in the light. No rash. No fever. No coryzal symptoms. Not woken up in the night. No tingling/numbness/weakness. 
Well before this episode. Has been lacking in energy but no wt loss. 
Severity 5-8/10. Taking ibuprofen, eases slightly but not resolving. 

Has had some generalised headaches before but not bad and associated symptoms/altered vision.
PMH: Nil
DH: Mercilon. BP last checked 1 yr ago was normal.

FMHx: Mum has migraine.

SH: Non smoker. No illicit drugs, 3 beers/week. Works from home.

Imp: Likely migraine but as ongoing and persistent needs neurological examination to exclude other pathology. Advised regular paracetamol and naproxen with food (will prescribe). If gets worse in meantime contact us straightaway or go to A&E is severe/unrelenting.

gold_sections.HIGHLIGHTS:
Tired
Did lots of walking in shorts in Yosemite
red circular rash
fever
sore throat
Aching shoulder/legs/back

naive.response_text:
Matt, I understand how concerning it must be to feel unwell for several weeks. The symptoms you're experiencing, including the rash and fatigue, are significant, and it's good that you're seeking answers. Based on your description, we considered potential causes such as Lyme disease, which can present with a rash and systemic symptoms. We've discussed the importance of testing to explore these possibilities and have a plan for follow-up to monitor your progress.

Please know that we're here to support you through this. The testing will help us better understand what's going on, and once we have those results, we can tailor our approach to your care. In the meantime, if you have any questions or concerns, don't hesitate to reach out. Together, we'll work towards finding the right solution for you. Take care, and I look forward to our next conversation.

strong_prompt.response_text:
Thank you for sharing all that with me, Matt. It sounds like you're feeling quite fatigued and run down, and I can understand how that would be concerning, especially when it's been going on for six weeks. The rash you noticed on your calf is also something to look into, and it's good you mentioned it—those rashes can sometimes be a clue to underlying issues.

Given your symptoms, we should consider a few possibilities. The rash could be related to a tick bite, which might suggest Lyme disease, a condition we can test for [PMID ?]. Additionally, prolonged fatigue could be linked to a viral infection or other systemic issues. To get a clearer picture, I recommend some blood tests to check for Lyme disease, infections, and other potential causes of your fatigue.

We'll plan to follow up in a week after the tests to review the results. In the meantime, if you notice any new symptoms like fevers or significant worsening, please reach out sooner. Before we wrap up, could you summarize what we've agreed on today? That way, I can ensure everything is clear.
